source_paper_title: Positivstellensätze and Moment Problems with Universal Quantifiers
context:
Section 7 (Conclusions and Discussions), page 26

"Our work leads to many intriguing questions to explore in the future. For instance, without assuming archimedeanness, is there a preordering version of Theorem 3.4? Equivalently, does there exist a clean algebraic reformulation of the compactness (or emptiness) of the set K given with a universal quantifier as in (1.1)? Is there an analog of the Krivine-Stengle Positivstellensatz for such sets K?"
title: Preordering/Krivine–Stengle-type Positivstellensatz and algebraic compactness certificates for universally quantified sets
latex:
Let \(n,m,s\in\mathbb{N}\). Let \(Q\subseteq\mathbb{R}^m\) be a closed set, and let \(g_1,\dots,g_s\in\mathbb{R}[x,y]\) be polynomials in \(x\in\mathbb{R}^n\) and \(y\in\mathbb{R}^m\). Define the universally quantified feasible set
\[
K\;:=\;\{x\in\mathbb{R}^n:\ g_j(x,y)\ge 0\ \text{for all}\ y\in Q\ \text{and all}\ j=1,\dots,s\}.
\]
Fix a finite nonnegative Borel measure \(\nu\) on \(\mathbb{R}^m\) with \(\mathrm{supp}(\nu)=Q\) that satisfies the multivariate Carleman condition
\[
\sum_{d=1}^{\infty}\left(\int y_j^{2d}\,d\nu(y)\right)^{-\frac{1}{2d}}=\infty\quad\text{for}\ j=1,\dots,m.
\]
Define the quadratic module
\[
\mathrm{QM}[g,\nu]
:=
\left\{\sum_{j=0}^s \int \tau_j(x,y)\,g_j(x,y)\,d\nu(y):\ \tau_j\in\Sigma^2[x,y],\ g_0\equiv 1\right\}\subseteq\mathbb{R}[x].
\]
Assume \(\mathrm{QM}[g,\nu]\) is not archimedean (equivalently, \(K\) may be unbounded).

Problem: Determine whether there exists a “preordering” (Schmüdgen/Krivine–Stengle-type) Positivstellensatz for \(K\) that does not assume archimedeanness, i.e., find a natural cone \(\mathcal{P}[g,\nu]\subseteq\mathbb{R}[x]\) generated by \(g\) and \(\nu\) in a preordering-like way such that for every \(f\in\mathbb{R}[x]\), strict positivity \(f(x)>0\) for all \(x\in K\) implies \(f\in\mathcal{P}[g,\nu]\), and similarly give an explicit algebraic certificate characterizing compactness or emptiness of \(K\) in terms of membership of \(\mathcal{P}[g,\nu]\) (e.g., analogs of \(-1\in\mathcal{P}[g,\nu]\) for emptiness).
open_problem_quote: "Our work leads to many intriguing questions to explore in the future. For instance, without assuming archimedeanness, is there a preordering version of Theorem 3.4? Equivalently, does there exist a clean algebraic reformulation of the compactness (or emptiness) of the set K given with a universal quantifier as in (1.1)? Is there an analog of the Krivine-Stengle Positivstellensatz for such sets K?"

verification.notice: This entry was generated from the paper text only. It has not been independently verified as still open. If you believe the question is solved or incorrectly stated, please contact the maintainers.
